summary: Large item-system decomp pass that raised every item translation unit targeted by the PR above 60% fuzzy match and added new linkages. The PR implemented or improved many item functions across Ness yoyo, Tingle, Hinoarashi/Cyndaquil, Flipper, Kyasarin, Freeze, Pikachu Thunder/Jolt Ground, Yoshi Egg Throw, Kirby 2F23, and related headers. Four item source files were promoted from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py: itfreeze.c, itpikachutjoltground.c, ithinoarashi.c, and ittincle.c. The decomp.dev bot reported +10,876 matched-code bytes, +15,096 linked-code bytes, 48 new matches, and 18 improvements in still-unmatched item units.

key_files.role: Promoted itfreeze.c, itpikachutjoltground.c, ithinoarashi.c, and ittincle.c from NonMatching to Matching.
Adjusted item function/data symbol scope and local labels, especially strings and sdata/sdata2 constants, to support the new linkages.
Largest source change; added substantial Ness yoyo decomp work and improved multiple still-fuzzy functions.
Major Tingle item implementation work; bot reported multiple new 100% matches including it_802ECA70, itTincle_UnkMotion2_Phys, it_802EC870, it_802EBFAC, it_802EB6DC, it_802EB870, and it_802EC69C.
Implemented Hinoarashi item logic and item state tables; promoted to Matching.
Added Flipper behavior functions and helper inlines; bot reported new/improved matches for it_80290A7C, it_80291344, damage callbacks, and related logic.
Added large Kyasarin decomp work; bot reported new 100% matches for itKyasarin_UnkMotion8_Anim and itKyasarin_UnkMotion6_Anim and a 98.14% improvement for it_802EDDC0.
Implemented Freeze motion/collision logic and data; promoted to Matching.
Implemented Pikachu thunder-jolt-ground callbacks and data; promoted to Matching.
Improved Pikachu Thunder functions and data; bot reported near-matches for itPikachuthunder_UnkMotion2_Anim and it_802B1DF8.
Added Yoshi Egg Throw functions/data, including new/improved logic callbacks.
Refined common item attributes/item-vars for Kyasarin, Tingle, Oldottosea, and Yoshi Egg Throw.
Refined character item structs for Pikachu Jolt Ground and Hinoarashi and added itNessYoyo_ItemVars.
Typed yoyo DAT attributes as HSD_Joint and HSD_MatAnimJoint pointers instead of generic s32 fields.
Small shared type/header adjustment supporting item decomp work.

what_changed: Converted four item translation units in configure.py from NonMatching to Matching: itfreeze.c, itpikachutjoltground.c, ithinoarashi.c, and ittincle.c.
Added 2,169 lines and removed 262 lines across 24 files, mostly item .c implementations and item struct headers.
Replaced many placeholder function markers such as /// #it_80290A7C, /// #itFreeze_UnkMotion4_Coll, and /// #it_802F258C with C implementations or helper-inlined implementations.
Added or completed item state tables in several files, including Freeze, Hinoarashi, and Kyasarin areas shown in the diff excerpt.

Refined header layouts by replacing padding with typed fields: examples include itPikachutJoltGround_ItemVars.xDD8 as HSD_GObj*, itPikachutJoltGroundAttributes.x0 as f32, Hinoarashi extra f32/Vec3 fields, itTincle_ItemVars and itTincleAttributes fields, itOldottosea_ItemVars.x20 as Item_GObj*, and new itYoshiEggThrowAttributes.
Added itNessYoyo_ItemVars in itCharItems.h with ItemLink*, HSD_GObj*, and HSD_JObj* fields.
Changed itYoyoAttributes fields x50/x54/x58 from generic s32 unknowns to HSD_Joint* string/yoyo joint pointers and HSD_MatAnimJoint* yoyo material animation pointer.
Cleaned GALE01 symbols by changing several functions/data objects from global to local scope and replacing address-style globals with @NNN local labels for strings, floats, and doubles.
smart_moves: Promoted files to Matching only where the build config showed complete TU-level matching evidence, rather than leaving them as partially linked NonMatching units.

Used struct/header refinement to unlock source-level expression matching: padding was replaced with typed pointers, floats, Vec3s, ItemLink pointers, and item attribute fields where code needed meaningful accesses.
Used static inline helper functions in item files such as Flipper, Freeze, Kyasarin, and Kirby 2F23 to share repeated logic while preserving codegen-sensitive call structure.
Adjusted symbol scope from global to local for compiler-local constants and strings, reducing artificial global linkage and aligning the symbol map with generated object structure.
Kept unknown address-style names where semantic names were not proven, while adding typed fields where the diff had enough evidence from use sites.
decomp_lessons: For item TUs, matching often depends as much on correct item-var and attribute struct layouts as on rewriting function bodies; replacing padding with typed fields can cascade into many function matches.
When item attributes point into HSD resource data, use HSD_Joint*, HSD_JObj*, or HSD_MatAnimJoint* types if use sites prove the resource type; generic s32 placeholders can block clean matching.
Local compiler constants in .data, .sdata, and .sdata2 should usually be represented as @NNN local symbols rather than exported it_804Dxxxx globals when they are not referenced globally.

Small callback functions and ItemStateTable entries should be decompiled together with their state table data; completing the table can provide linkage evidence for otherwise anonymous motion callbacks.
Promoting an object to Matching in configure.py is a useful final validation step but should be paired with symbol scope cleanup so the linked-code and linked-data metrics move together.

When a function is not yet fully matching, bot fuzzy percentages still identify high-value follow-up targets; this PR left several near-complete functions in Ness yoyo, Kyasarin, Flipper, Pikachu Thunder, and Yoshi Egg Throw.
naming_conventions: Kept existing item file/module prefixes such as itFreeze_, itFlipper_, itHinoarashi_, itKyasarin_, itPikachutjoltground_, itPikachuthunder_, itTincle_, and itYoshieggthrow_.
Used UnkMotionN_Anim, UnkMotionN_Phys, and UnkMotionN_Coll suffixes for item state callbacks when semantic motion names were not established.
Used LogicNN callback names such as it_2725_Logic106_DmgDealt, ShieldBounced, Reflected, Clanked, and DmgReceived for item interaction callbacks.
Used offset-based field names such as xDD8, x0, x4, x6C, and x78 when semantics remained unknown, but changed field types to match proven usage.
Used @NNN labels in symbols.txt for local compiler-emitted constants/strings rather than address-derived it_80xxxxxx global names.
For yoyo resource attributes, introduced semantic field suffixes x50_string_joint, x54_yoyo_joint, and x58_yoyo_matanim where the diff showed enough evidence.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Static inline helpers were used to influence code generation and reuse repeated code without necessarily creating standalone symbols; examples in the diff include it_80290CE8_inline, it_80290DD4_inline, it_80291344_inline, itFreeze_ClearLinkedItem, itFreeze_ResetToMotion0, it_802F258C_scale, it_802F258C_update, and itKyasarin_TurnAround.
Stack-shape padding tactics appear in the diff, including explicit u8 _pad[8] locals and PAD_STACK(8) in Flipper code.
Math-library calls and macros were used in item physics reconstruction, including sqrtf__Ff, atan2f, ABS, M_PI, and M_PI_2.
Several object/data symbols were marked local in config/GALE01/symbols.txt to match compiler-local data emission and improve data/linkage matching.

Item code consistently used project access macros such as GET_ITEM and GET_JOBJ and existing HSD setters such as HSD_JObjSetRotationX, HSD_JObjSetRotationY, HSD_JObjSetRotationZ, and HSD_JObjSetScale.

review_feedback.summary: GALE01 report showed matched code 63.74% (+0.28%, +10876 bytes), linked code 35.40% (+0.39%, +15096 bytes), matched data 38.25% (+0.08%, +1008 bytes), linked data 32.58% (+0.06%, +712 bytes), 48 new matches, and 18 improvements in unmatched items.
No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish itnessyoyo functions it_802BFAFC, it_802BF4A0, it_802BE65C, it_802BF28C, and it_802BE9D8.
Search for subsequent renames of itTincle, itHinoarashi, itKyasarin, and itFlipper UnkMotion callbacks after this matching pass.
Check whether the typed itYoyoAttributes HSD_Joint and HSD_MatAnimJoint fields were reused or renamed in later yoyo/Ness item work.
Look for later PRs that convert remaining near-matches in itkyasarin, itflipper, itpikachuthunder, and ityoshieggthrow to full matches.
Audit symbols.txt changes around @NNN local labels to ensure no later PR re-globalized the same sdata2/data constants.
